Requirement or Standard: No person shall allow a public nuisance including filth, garbage, decayed food, vegetation overgrowth, animal/human excrement, mold, or biological fluids.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Premises must be free of all declared nuisances; materials set out for collection in compliance with Sec. 283.

Requirement or Standard: Buildings and premises must be kept continuously clean and free from rodents; seal all structural openings larger than  $1/4$  inch ( $0.25$  inches).
Compliance/Ready Standard: Use metal, hardware cloth, or concrete; no caulk or foam. Maintain building walls to prevent access to food.

Requirement or Standard: Owners must investigate complaints within 72 hours (or 2 working days) and provide 2-year infestation/abatement history to prospective tenants.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Written disclosure signed by both parties prior to possession; 28-day clear monitoring period required for abatement.
Approved Wording or Legal Source: SF Health Code Art. 11A; Dir. Rules Sec. 1.11, 2.4

Requirement or Standard: Provide at least one rigid plastic or metal container with a tight-fitting, watertight lid per dwelling unit.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Containers must be rodent-proof and insect-proof; frequent collections to prevent over-filling.
Approved Wording or Legal Source: SF Health Code Art. 6 Sec. 290; Reg. VII(1)

Requirement or Standard: Lack of improper water closet, lavatory, bathtub, or shower. Minimum hot water storage of 4 gallons per guest room / 8 gallons per unit.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Temperature at tap 105 to 120 degrees Fahrenheit; fixtures must be within the unit or for exclusive use.

Requirement or Standard: Address visible mold, musty odors, and structural leaks; SFDPH intervenes if mold area exceeds 10 square feet.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Fix leaks in pipes/roofs; maintain indoor humidity below 55% ( $40-50$ % via hygrometer). Never paint over mold.
Approved Wording or Legal Source: SF Health Code Sec. 581(b)(6); Dir. Rules

Requirement or Standard: Maintain tree and shrub branches at least 3 feet away from or above buildings.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Clear space of 24 inches along fences/walls; no dense groundcover hiding burrows.

Requirement or Standard: Eliminate all standing water (bird baths, gutters, tires) to prevent West Nile virus; install 16-mesh screens on ventilation.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Change bird bath water weekly; chlorinate pools; dry soil between waterings.

Requirement or Standard: Maintain minimum room temperature of 70 degrees Fahrenheit in all habitable rooms.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Measured at a point 3 feet above the floor.

Requirement or Standard: Lead hazards within control of owner are prohibited; paint on structures built before 1979 is presumed lead-based.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Abatement of lead hazards as defined in Section 1603.

Requirement or Standard: Replace showerheads exceeding 1.8 gallons per minute and water closets exceeding 1.28 gallons per flush.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Comply with SF Plumbing Code Section 402.2 and CA Energy Commission standards.

Requirement or Standard: Resident caretaker required for apartment houses with 16+ units or hotels with 12+ rooms; 135-degree door viewers required.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Caretaker must reside on premises; viewers mounted at 58 inches or less.

Requirement or Standard: Annual program fee for buildings with 3 or more rental units (e.g., 3 units is 25, over 30 units is 103).
Compliance/Ready Standard: 3-unit buildings are exempt if owner-occupied; late penalty of 10 after 30 days; 30 after 60 days.

Requirement or Standard: Owners shall pay a fee for reinspections to verify correction of violations (FY24-25: 167 per hour; FY26-27: 437 per hour).
Compliance/Ready Standard: Payment within 30 days or face a 40 late penalty; interest accrues at 1.5% per month.

Requirement or Standard: Violation of fire, heat, or hot water is a misdemeanor; fines for code violations range from 100 to 1000 per day.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Criminal misdemeanor or administrative penalties assessed based on non-compliance with Notice of Violation.

Requirement or Standard: Written notice must be provided to owners and tenants before pesticide application.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Must include "CAUTION" text, active ingredients, and Poison Control number; 48-hour notice for fumigation.

Requirement or Standard: Served within 15 days of determination; must include violations, deadline, and penalty info.
Compliance/Ready Standard: General violations must be corrected within 30 days; sewage hazards within 48-72 hours.

Requirement or Standard: Tenants must notify landlord in writing and allow 72 hours for a response before reporting to 311.
Compliance/Ready Standard: A tenant understands what to do before reporting and when to use 311.

Requirement or Standard: All public-facing content must be written at an 8th-grade reading level or lower (Grade 5-6 for instructions).
Compliance/Ready Standard: Use plain language; replace "shall" with "must"; use "rats or mice" instead of "rodents" in titles.

Requirement or Standard: All digital content must comply with WCAG 2.1 Level AA and ADA Title II.
Compliance/Ready Standard: HTML-first design; sequential header hierarchy (H1-H2-H3); minimum 4.5:1 color contrast; descriptive link text.

Requirement or Standard: Every page must use an approved Karl CMS type: Topic, Transaction, Information, Step-by-step, About, or Resource.
Compliance/Ready Standard: No mixed-task pages (e.g., Information + Transaction); no placeholder/under-construction pages.

Requirement or Standard: Meta Title under 60 chars; Meta Description between 120 and 160 chars (starting with active verb).
Compliance/Ready Standard: The meta description must be under 110 characters for specific transaction blueprints.

Requirement or Standard: The Three-Click Rule: users must reach any page from the parent hub in 3 clicks or fewer.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Standard top-to-bottom layout; no custom column grids; include a Table of Contents.

Requirement or Standard: Defines key terms used throughout Article 11, including City, Department, Director, Manager, Owner, Person, and Responsible Party.
Compliance/Ready Standard: No action needed; terms are used to interpret responsibilities elsewhere in the Article.

Requirement or Standard: Prohibits public nuisances such as filth, pest infestations, mold, spoiled food, lead hazards, and poison oak/ivy on any property.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Premises must be kept free of all 18 listed nuisance conditions at all times.

Requirement or Standard: Authorizes the Department to seize, condemn, and destroy spoiled or contaminated food and drink that pose a nuisance.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Owners must allow seizure and destruction of unsafe food once identified by the Department.

Section/Topic: Sec. 590 - Discharge of Soot, Smoke, etc.
Requirement or Standard: Bans dense smoke, soot, ash, or fume discharges from furnaces and flues in residential or commercial districts, with limited exceptions.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Emissions must stay below the Ringelmann Chart Diagram No. 3 density and equipment must be properly maintained.

Requirement or Standard: Sets a fine of up to $50 and/or five days in jail for violating the smoke and soot restrictions in Section 590.
Requires every hotel operator to obtain and annually renew a Certificate of Sanitation from the Department.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Certificate must be renewed yearly following a satisfactory reinspection; it is non-transferable upon sale or transfer.

Requirement or Standard: Defines "hotel" as any building with six or more guest rooms rented out for sleeping purposes.
Makes failure to hold a valid Certificate of Sanitation a misdemeanor, with each day counted as a separate offense.
Compliance/Ready Standard: N/A - Penalty provision; up to one year jail or $1,000 fine per violation.

Requirement or Standard: Requires the Department to inspect hotel premises for nuisances and sanitary conditions before issuing a sanitation certificate.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Owner must permit inspection of the premises prior to certificate issuance.

Section/Topic: Sec. 596 - Administrative Procedures Initiated with Notice of Violation
Requirement or Standard: Establishes the complaint, inspection, Notice of Violation, Order to Vacate, hearing, and Director's Order process used to abate nuisances.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Responsible Parties must generally correct a cited nuisance within 30 days of a Notice of Violation, absent an extension.

Section/Topic: Sec. 596.5 - Citations Under Admin Code Chapter 100
Requirement or Standard: Allows the Director to issue administrative citations as an alternative enforcement path under Administrative Code Chapter 100.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Responsible Party must correct the violation within the time stated in the written notice.

Requirement or Standard: Directs the Director to notify the Chief of Police so officers can assist in enforcing vacation orders.

Section/Topic: Sec. 598 - Penalty for Resisting Order to Vacate
Requirement or Standard: Makes it a misdemeanor to forcibly resist or prevent enforcement of a vacate order.
Compliance/Ready Standard: N/A - Penalty provision; fine of $100-$1,000 and/or up to three months jail.

Requirement or Standard: Sets out the lien, special assessment, and cost-recovery procedures the City uses to collect unpaid abatement costs from owners.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Owner must reimburse the Department's abatement costs or risk a property lien or special assessment.

Requirement or Standard: Establishes criminal, civil, and administrative penalties, up to $1,000 per violation per day, for violating Article 11's nuisance provisions.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Violations must be corrected within the notice period to avoid accruing penalties.

Section/Topic: Sec. 605 - Poison Ivy and Poison Oak, Removal on Notice
Requirement or Standard: Requires owners to remove poison oak or poison ivy growth on their property when ordered by the Director.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Poisonous growth must be removed and destroyed as directed by the Director.

Requirement or Standard: This section was repealed and no longer imposes any requirement.
Charges the Department with enforcing the poison oak and poison ivy removal requirement in Section 605.
Sets a misdemeanor penalty of up to $1,000 and/or 15 or more days in jail for failing to remove poisonous growth.

Section/Topic: Sec. 609 - Vector Control and Healthy Housing Inspection Program Fee
Requirement or Standard: Requires apartment house and hotel owners to pay an annual fee, based on unit count, funding vector-control and healthy-housing inspections.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Annual fee ranges from $25 (3 units) to $103 (30+ units) for apartments, and $151-$582 for hotels; late payment adds penalties.

Requirement or Standard: Authorizes hourly reinspection fees, $167/hr for an inspector and $150/hr for a technician, for follow-up inspections verifying correction.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Owner must pay the reinspection fee within 30 days or incur a $40 late penalty and possible lien.

Requirement or Standard: Directs the Controller to review and adjust the Section 609 and 609.1 fees annually to match program costs.
Allows the prevailing party to recover attorneys' fees in a nuisance abatement action if the Director elected to seek them.

Section/Topic: Sec. 613 - Operation of Gas Works Regulated
Requirement or Standard: Prohibits gas manufacturers from discharging harmful gas, tar, or refuse, or producing dangerously impure illuminating gas.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Operators must use best practices to prevent the escape of harmful odors or substances.

Requirement or Standard: Authorizes the Director to notify vacant-lot owners about voluntarily dedicating their land to gardens or park space.
Clarifies that "shall" or "must" language directed at the City creates a discretionary, not mandatory, duty.
States the City is not liable for damages from reliance on this Article, and that inspections are discretionary.
No Person shall have upon any premises or real property owned, occupied or controlled by him, or her, or it any public nuisance.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Owner/Occupant must not have any public nuisance on premises.

Requirement or Standard: Any accumulation of filth, garbage, decayed or spoiled food, unsanitary debris or waste material or decaying animal or vegetable matter unless set out for collection.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Must not accumulate filth, garbage, or decaying matter unless properly set for collection.

Requirement or Standard: Any accumulation of hay, grass, straw, weeds, or vegetation overgrowth.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Must prevent accumulation of hay, grass, weeds, and vegetation overgrowth.

Requirement or Standard: Any accumulation of waste paper, litter or combustible trash unless such materials are set out for collection.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Must not accumulate waste paper or combustible trash unless properly set for collection.

Requirement or Standard: Any buildings, structures, or portion thereof found to be unsanitary.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Buildings and structures must be maintained in a sanitary condition.

Requirement or Standard: Any matter or material which constitutes, or is contaminated by, animal or human excrement, urine or other biological fluids.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Must remove material contaminated by human or animal excrement or biological fluids.

Requirement or Standard: Any visible or otherwise demonstrable mold or mildew in the interiors of any buildings or facilities.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Must eliminate visible or demonstrable mold and mildew from building interiors.

Requirement or Standard: Any pest harborage or infestation including but not limited to pigeons, skunks, raccoons, opossums, and snakes, except lawful pigeon harborages.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Must prevent pest harborage and infestations of pigeons, skunks, raccoons, opossums, snakes.

Requirement or Standard: Any noxious insect harborage or infestation including cockroaches, bed bugs, fleas, scabies, lice, spiders, houseflies, wasps and mosquitoes.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Must eliminate noxious insect harborages and infestations including bed bugs and roaches.

Requirement or Standard: Any article of food or drink in the possession or under the control of any person which is tainted, decayed, spoiled or otherwise unwholesome.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Must not possess tainted, decayed, or unwholesome food or drink.

Requirement or Standard: Any vacant lots, open spaces, and other properties... which become infested with poison oak or poison ivy shrub.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Must prevent and remove poison oak and poison ivy from vacant lots and properties.

Requirement or Standard: Any violation of Section 37 of this Code; [Article 1].
Compliance/Ready Standard: Must comply with Section 37 limits and regulations on animals.

Requirement or Standard: Any violation of Section 92 of this Code; [Article 2].
Compliance/Ready Standard: Must comply with Section 92 (Rodent Control) maintaining sanitary conditions.

Requirement or Standard: Any violation of Section 590 of this Article.
Any violations of rules or regulations the Director adopts to implement the provisions of this Article or applicable provisions of State law.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Must comply with all implementing rules and regulations adopted by the Director.

Requirement or Standard: Anything else that the Director deems to be a threat to public health and safety.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Must abate any condition the Director deems a public health and safety threat.

Requirement or Standard: Every owner of an apartment house or hotel... shall pay an annual fee... determined by the number of rental units.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Owner must pay the annual Vector Control and Healthy Housing Inspection Program Fee.

Requirement or Standard: Director shall within 15 days cause a Notice of Violation to be served either personally or by first class mailing to the Responsible Parties.
Compliance/Ready Standard: N/A - Administrative procedure for serving notice to Responsible Parties.

Requirement or Standard: The Director shall specify in the Notice of Violation the time period within which the Responsible Party must abate the nuisance. Such time period shall not exceed 30 days...
Compliance/Ready Standard: Responsible Party must abate the nuisance within the specified time, not to exceed 30 days.

Requirement or Standard: If Owner/Responsible Parties fail to comply... Director may hold a Hearing or cause abatement... Owner shall be indebted to the City for all costs.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Owner must pay all abatement costs and fees if the City must abate the nuisance.

Requirement or Standard: Owner/Responsible Parties may be liable for other charges, costs, administrative costs, expenses... fines, attorneys' fees, and penalties.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Owner is liable for additional charges, fines, penalties, and attorneys' fees.

Requirement or Standard: Duty of Department upon application... to cause premises to be inspected for purpose of ascertaining whether said premises are free of nuisances and sanitary.
Compliance/Ready Standard: Owner must permit inspection to verify premises are sanitary and free of nuisances.

Requirement or Standard: Whenever a written or oral complaint is made... the Director shall inspect the building, structure or property to verify the existence of a nuisance.
Compliance/Ready Standard: N/A - Administrative mandate to inspect upon complaint.

Requirement or Standard: "City" shall mean the City and County of San Francisco.
"Department" shall mean the San Francisco Department of Public Health.
"Director" shall mean the Director of Public Health or his or her designee.
"Manager" shall mean the authorized agent for the Owner... responsible for the day-to-day operation.
"Owner" shall mean any Person who possesses, has title to or an interest in, harbors or has control, custody or possession...
"Person" shall mean and include corporations, estates, associations, partnerships and trusts, individual human beings...
"Responsible Party" shall include the Owner, Manager, tenant, or any Person having control over a property or who creates or allows a nuisance.
